title: erucastrum obtusangulum subsp. intermedium
intro: Erucastrum obtusangulum subsp. intermedium, also known as the intermediate dog's mustard, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family and is native to certain regions of Europe. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Erucastrum obtusangulum subsp. intermedium include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or added to salads to harness its medicinal and nutritional properties.
